frameworks.name: GABAergic intracortical inhibition
frameworks.one_line: Cortical excitation/inhibition balance modulated by tDCS, indexed psychophysically.

frameworks.name: surround suppression
frameworks.one_line: Psychophysical index of V1 intracortical inhibition.

frameworks.name: overlay suppression
frameworks.one_line: LGN-origin suppression used as a non-cortical control measure.

core_question: Can anodal tDCS reduce intracortical inhibition in the human visual cortex as measured psychophysically?
key_assumptions: Surround suppression indexes V1 intracortical (GABA-mediated) inhibition.
Overlay suppression originates in the LGN and is non-cortical.
Anodal tDCS reduces GABA-mediated inhibition as shown previously in motor cortex.
manipulations: Stimulation polarity (anodal vs cathodal) over occipital poles.
key_findings: Anodal occipital tDCS significantly reduced psychophysical surround suppression.
Anodal tDCS had no effect on overlay suppression.
Cathodal stimulation had no effect on either measure.
interpretations: tDCS can reduce intracortical inhibition within human visual cortex, generalizing beyond motor cortex.
Effect is polarity-specific and localized to cortical (not subcortical) suppression.
